reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. The triangular numbers are defined as {t : ℤ | ∃ n : ℤ, t ≥ 0 ∧ t = n(n+1)/2}; though the informal uses n ∈ ℕ, using n ∈ ℤ yields the same set since n(n+1)/2 = (-n-1)(-n)/2, and n(n+1)/2 ≥ 0 for all integers n (so the t ≥ 0 is redundant but harmless). Integer division works exactly because n(n+1) is always even. The condition ∀ t > 0 on ℤ matches "every positive integer t", the iff matches "if and only if", and `.encard = ⊤` correctly encodes "infinitely many" (by Set.encard_eq_top_iff ↔ Infinite). The ambient type ℤ × ℤ matches "ordered pairs of integers".
